```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC transformation: anti‑diagonal mirroring + one‑cell padding
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Input facts are supplied by the harness (input/3).
%  Output must be produced as output/3 with the same colour domain.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Maximum row and column indices (0‑based)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
max_row(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
max_col(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Size of the padded square: max(rowCount, colCount)
%    rowCount = Rmax+1, colCount = Cmax+1
% ------------------------------------------------------------
outSize(S) :-
    max_row(Rmax),
    max_col(Cmax),
    S = #max { Rmax + 1, Cmax + 1 }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Output domain: all cells of the padded square
% ------------------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R,C) :- R = 0..outSize-1,
                  C = 0..outSize-1.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Transformation rule (mirror over the anti‑diagonal)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,V) :-
    outSize(S),
    input(R0,C0,V),
    V != 0,
    R = S - C0,
    C = S - R0.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Exactly one colour per output cell (choice)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Background (colour 0) for cells that stay empty after the rule
% ------------------------------------------------------------
has_color(R,C) :- output(R,C,V), V != 0.

output(R,C,0) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    not has_color(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Ensure that all output cells are defined (helps grounding)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
:- not output_cell(_, _).
```

```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC transformation: anti‑diagonal mirroring with padding
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% The input predicate is supplied automatically (input/3).
% The colour domain is 0..9 (colour/1 facts are supplied).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Compute the bounding box and the size of the smallest square
%  that contains all input cells (including the zeros).
% ------------------------------------------------------------
maxRow(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
maxCol(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

% Compute size of the minimal square (0‑based indices)
grid(S) :-
    maxRow(Rmax),
    maxCol(Cmax),
    S = #max { Rmax, Cmax } + 1.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Domain for the padded square (rows 0..S‑1, cols 0..S‑1).
% ------------------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R,C) :- grid(S), R = 0..S-1, C = 0..S-1.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Mirror every non‑zero input cell over the anti‑diagonal.
%  The anti‑diagonal of a square of size S has coordinates
%  (S‑1‑c , S‑1‑r).
% ------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,V) :-
    grid(S),
    input(R0,C0,V),
    V != 0,
    R = S-1 - C0,
    C = S-1 - R0.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Helper predicate: a cell that already has a non‑zero colour.
% ------------------------------------------------------------
hasNonZeroCell(R,C,V) :- output(R,C,V), colour(V), V != 0.

% At least one colour makes hasNonZero(R,C) true.
hasNonZero(R,C) :- hasNonZeroCell(R,C,V).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Cells that never receive a non‑zero colour stay 0.
% ------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,0) :- output_cell(R,C), not hasNonZero(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Exactly one colour (0..9) must be assigned to every output cell.
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,V) : colour(V) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  End of program
% ------------------------------------------------------------
```

```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC transformation – anti‑diagonal mirroring with one‑cell padding
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% 1. Determine max row and column indices of input cells.
max_row(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{R : input(R,_,_) }.
max_col(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{C : input(_,C,_) }.

% 2. Compute grid size: max of extents + 2.
%    First compute the larger extent (S0), then final size (S).
grid(S) :-
    max_row(Rmax),
    max_col(Cmax),
    S0 = #max{Rmax, Cmax},
    S = S0 + 2.

% 3. Positions inside the padded square.
output_cell(R,C) :-
    grid(S),
    R = 0..S-1,
    C = 0..S-1.

% 4. Mirror each non‑zero input cell across the anti‑diagonal.
mirror(R,C,V) :-
    grid(S),
    input(R0,C0,V),
    V != 0,
    R = S-1 - C0,
    C = S-1 - R0.

output(R,C,V) :- mirror(R,C,V), V != 0.

% 5. Cells that never receive a non‑zero colour stay 0.
output(R,C,0) :- output_cell(R,C), not mirror(R,C,_).

% 6. Prevent a cell from receiving both zero and a non‑zero colour.
:- output(R,C,0), output(R,C,V), V != 0.

% 7. Exactly one colour (from colour/1) per output cell.
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  End of program.
% ------------------------------------------------------------
```

```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC transformation – anti‑diagonal mirroring with padding
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% ---------- size of the padded square ----------
maxRow(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
maxCol(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

paddedSize(S) :-
    maxRow(Rmax),
    maxCol(Cmax),
    RowCount = Rmax + 1,
    ColCount = Cmax + 1,
    maxRC = #max { RowCount, ColCount },
    S = maxRC + 1.

% ---------- cells of the padded square ----------
cell(R,C) :- paddedSize(S), R = 0..S-1, C = 0..S-1.

% ---------- mirror each non‑zero input cell ----------
output(R,C,V) :-
    paddedSize(S),
    input(R0,C0,V),
    V != 0,
    R = S-1 - C0,
    C = S-1 - R0.

% ---------- helper predicate ----------
has_mirror(R,C) :- output(R,C,V), V != 0.

% ---------- background colour (0) for cells without a mirror ----------
output(R,C,0) :-
    cell(R,C),
    not has_mirror(R,C).

% ---------- at most one colour per cell ----------
:- output(R,C,V), output(R,C,W), V != W.

% ---------- forbid a non‑zero colour where no mirror exists ----------
:- output(R,C,V), V != 0, not has_mirror(R,C).
```

```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC – anti‑diagonal mirroring with padding (tiling version)
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. All needed bounds, counts and derived sizes.
%    They are placed in a single rule so that every variable
%    that appears in an aggregate also appears positively
%    elsewhere in the same rule (no unsafe variables).
% ------------------------------------------------------------
init(RmaxNZ,RminNZ,CmaxNZ,CminNZ,Sh,Sw,S,
      RmaxAll,RmaxAllIdx,CmaxAll,CmaxAllIdx,SO,B) :-
    RmaxNZ = #max { R : input(R,_,V), V != 0 },
    RminNZ = #min { R : input(R,_,V), V != 0 },
    CmaxNZ = #max { C : input(_,C,V), V != 0 },
    CminNZ = #min { C : input(_,C,V), V != 0 },
    Sh = RmaxNZ - RminNZ + 1,
    Sw = CmaxNZ - CminNZ + 1,
    S  = #max { X : X = Sh, X = Sw },
    RmaxAll  = #max { R : input(R,_,_) },
    CmaxAll  = #max { C : input(_,C,_) },
    RmaxAllIdx = RmaxAll + 1,
    CmaxAllIdx = CmaxAll + 1,
    SO = #max { X : X = RmaxAllIdx, X = CmaxAllIdx },
    B = SO / S.                     % integer division (B > 0 follows)

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 2. Block positions – require B>0 positively (safe negation)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
:- B > 0.

blockPos(0,0).                     % start at the top‑left corner
blockPos(i,j) :- i = 0..B-2, j = i + 1.   % (0,1),(1,2),…
blockPos(B-1,0).                   % last block (B‑1,0)

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. Copy the shape into every block on the anti‑diagonal path
% ------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,V) :-
    input(r,c,V), V != 0,
    blockPos(i,j),
    R = i * S + r,
    C = j * S + c.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. Cells of the padded square
% ------------------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R,C) :-
    R = 0..SO-1,
    C = 0..SO-1.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. Helper predicates
% ------------------------------------------------------------
has_nonzero(R,C) :- output(R,C,V), V != 0.

output(R,C,0) :- output_cell(R,C), not has_nonzero(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 6. Exactly one colour per cell
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 7. Consistency constraints
% ------------------------------------------------------------
:- output(R,C,V), output(R,C,W), V != W.
:- output(R,C,0), output(R,C,V), V != 0.
```

```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC – anti‑diagonal mirroring with one‑cell padding
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%
%  Input:    input/3  (the 8×8 diagram)
%  Colour:   colour/1 (0..9)
%  Output:   output/3 for every cell of the final 8×8 grid.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  1. size of the padded square (always 8, but compute it)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
max_row(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
max_col(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.
size(S) :- max_row(Rmax), max_col(Cmax), S = #max { Rmax + 1, Cmax + 1, 8 }.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  2. cells of the padded square
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
cell(R,C) :- size(S), R = 0..S-1, C = 0..S-1.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  3. copy the original (non‑zero) cell
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
orig(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  4. copy the mirrored (anti‑diagonal) cell
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
mir(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0, R = 7 - C0, C = 7 - R0.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  5. a cell that receives a non‑zero colour from either copy
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
has_nonzero(R,C) :- orig(R,C,V), mir(R,C,V), V != 0.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  6. background colour 0 for cells without a non‑zero copy
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not input(R,C,_).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  7. exactly one colour per cell (including 0)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,V) : colour(V) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  8. grounding (helps the solver)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
:- cell(R,C).
```

```asp
% Determine size of the padded square.
maxRow(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
maxCol(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.
size(S) :-
    maxRow(Rmax),
    maxCol(Cmax),
    S = #max { X : X = Rmax + 1, X = Cmax + 1 }.

% Cells of the final square
cell(R,C) :- size(S), R = 0..S-1, C = 0..S-1.

% Original non‑zero input cells
output(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Mirror of a non‑zero input cell over the anti‑diagonal
mir(R,c,V) :-
    size(S),
    input(r,c,V), V != 0,
    R = S-1 - c,
    C = S-1 - r.

output(R,C,V) :-
    mir(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Zero colour for every cell
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C).

% Exactly one colour per cell
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% Forbid zero when a non‑zero colour is present
:- output(R,C,0), output(R,C,V), V != 0.
```

```asp
maxRow(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
```

rewrite_details.system_prompt:
You are a Clingo/ASP syntax expert. Fix ONLY syntax errors — never change program logic or semantics.

# Common Clingo Syntax Errors

1. **No `aggregate/3`** — Use `N = #count { C : pred(C) }`, not `aggregate(N = #count, pred(C), N)`. The `aggregate/3` predicate is SWI-Prolog; it does not exist in Clingo.

2. **No `#mod`** — Use `\` for modulo: `C \ 2 = 0`, not `C #mod 2 = 0`. Clingo follows ISO Prolog arithmetic.

3. **Aggregate must be on the right-hand side** — Write `Var = #min { R : p(R) }`, not `#min { R : p(R) } = Var`. Clingo evaluates `Var = #aggr{...}`, not the reverse.

4. **`#const` names must be lowercase** — Write `#const max_dim = 30.`, not `#const MAX = 30.`. An uppercase token is parsed as a variable, which is invalid in a `#const` directive.

5. **No `exactly N` suffix on cardinality** — Write `1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- cell(R,C).`, not `{ ... } exactly 1 :- cell(R,C).`. Clingo uses prefix/suffix integer bounds.

6. **Unsafe variables under negation** — Every variable must appear in at least one positive body literal. Add a positive binder before negating: `missing(R) :- row(R), col(C), not input(R,C,5).`, not `missing(R) :- col(C), not input(R,C,5).` (here `R` is unsafe).

7. **No predicate names in arithmetic** — `row_min` is a predicate, not an integer. Extract the value first: `border(R) :- row_min(Rmin), R = Rmin - 1.`, not `border(R) :- R = row_min - 1.`

8. **No top-level variable assignment** — Aggregates must appear in a rule with a head: `min_row(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min { R : p(R) }.`, not the standalone `MinR = #min { R : p(R) }.`

9. **No `succ/2`, `plus/3`, `msort/2`** — These are SWI-Prolog. Use arithmetic directly: `Next = N + 1`, `Sum = A + B`. There is no sort predicate in Clingo.

10. **`#count` body needs curly braces with colon** — Write `N = #count { X : pred(X) }`, not `N = #count(pred(X))`. The aggregate body uses `{ Vars : condition }` syntax.

11. **Aggregate variables must be grounded** — In `N = #count { R,C : input(R,C,_) }`, the grounding set must be finite. All variables in the tuple must appear positively in the condition.

12. **Range syntax uses `..` not `to`** — Write `R = 0..H-1`, not `R = 0 to H-1`. Range expressions always use double dot.

13. **No `assert/retract`** — Clingo programs are static. There is no assert or retract. All rules are declared upfront.

14. **Conditional literals need correct position** — `output(R,C,V) : input(R,C,V)` is a conditional literal, valid inside aggregates or choice heads. Not valid as a standalone rule body.

15. **Head disjunction uses `;`** — `a ; b :- body.` means either a or b (or both) must hold. This is different from `1 { a ; b } 1 :- body.` which is a choice with exactly one.

16. **No inline aggregate-to-aggregate comparison** — You cannot compare two aggregates directly in a rule body. Bind at least one to a variable first: `pivot(P) :- col(P), N = #count{R : row(R)}, N = #count{R : input(R,P,5)}.`, not `pivot(P) :- col(P), #count{R : input(R,P,5)} = #count{R : row(R)}.`. Alternatively, avoid the aggregate entirely with negation-as-failure: `bad(P) :- col(P), row(R), not input(R,P,5). pivot(P) :- col(P), not bad(P).`

# Correct Pattern Reference

```asp
% Computing grid dimensions
height(H) :- H = 1 + #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
width(W)  :- W = 1 + #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

% Domain for output cells (same size as input)
output_cell(R,C) :- height(H), width(W), R = 0..H-1, C = 0..W-1.

% Choice rule (exactly one color per cell)
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% Count non-zero cells in a row
nonzero_count(R, N) :- N = #count { C : input(R,C,V), V != 0 }, input(R,_,_).

% Minimum row containing color 5
min_row_5(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min { R : input(R,_,5) }.

% Safe negation (bind R and C positively first)
empty(R,C) :- output_cell(R,C), not input(R,C,_).

% Modulo arithmetic
even_col(C) :- output_cell(_,C), C \ 2 = 0.

% Using a computed value in arithmetic
right_edge(R,C) :- width(W), output_cell(R,C), C = W - 1.
```

Common Clingo syntax fixes (apply immediately):
  aggregate(N=#count,cond,N)    →  N = #count { _ : cond }
  aggregate_all(#count,cond,N)  →  N = #count { _ : cond }
  C #mod 2                      →  C \ 2
  #min{...} = Var               →  Var = #min{...}
  { } exactly N :-              →  N { } N :-
  #count(...)                   →  #count{...}
  #sum(...)                     →  #sum{...}

Program:
```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% ARC – anti‑diagonal mirroring with padding
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% The harness supplies:
%   input/3  – every cell of the 9×9 diagram (colours 0..9)
%   colour/1 – colour domain
%   output/3 – must be produced.
%
% The transformation that matches the three examples:
%   1) the original cell,
%   2) a copy shifted 3 columns to the right,
%   3) a copy shifted 6 rows downwards,
%   4) a copy shifted 6 rows downwards and 3 columns to the right.
%   The shape also appears once on the opposite side (for shapes near
%   the bottom) by copying the shape, shifting it up by the minimal
%   row index of a non‑zero cell.
%
% The program below enforces exactly one colour per cell and avoids
% unsafe‑variable warnings.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Compute the size of the smallest square containing all cells
maxRow(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
maxCol(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.
size(S)    :- maxRow(Rmax), maxCol(Cmax), S = #max { X : X = Rmax+1, X = Cmax+1 }.

% All positions of the padded square
cell(R,C) :- size(S), R = 0..S-1, C = 0..S-1.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Original cells (keep the colour)
orig(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% 2. Column shift (+3)
col_shift(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                   R = R0,
                   C = C0 + 3,
                   C <= S-1.

% 3. Row shift (+ (S‑1‑maxRow)) – moves the shape towards the
%    opposite side when the shape is near the top.
shiftRow(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                  R = R0 + (S-1 - maxRow_nonzero),
                  C = C0,
                  R <= S-1.

% 4. Column shift followed by a row shift (+ (S‑1‑maxRow), +3)
col_shift_second(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                         R = R0 + (S-1 - maxRow_nonzero),
                         C = C0 + 3,
                         R <= S-1,
                         C <= S-1.

% 5. Top‑band copy (used when the shape is near the bottom)
Rmin(Rmin) :- #min { R : input(R,_,V), V != 0 }.
top_band(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                  R = R0 - Rmin,
                  C = C0 + 3,
                  R >= 0.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Source predicate – any transformation that places a non‑zero colour
source(R,C,V) :- orig(R,C,V), V != 0.
source(R,C,V) :- col_shift(R,C,V),   V != 0.
source(R,C,V) :- row_shift(R,C,V),   V != 0.
source(R,C,V) :- col_shift_second(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Does the cell have any non‑zero colour?
has_nonzero(R,C) :- source(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Non‑zero colour for cells that have a source
output(R,C,V) :- has_nonzero(R,C), source(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Background colour for cells without a source
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not has_nonzero(R,C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Uniqueness constraints
:- output(R,C,V), output(R,C,W), V != W.                % one colour per cell
:- source(R,C,V), source(R,C,W), V != W.               % at most one source colour per cell
:- output(R,C,0), output(R,C,V), V != 0.               % no zero + non‑zero in the same cell
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Grounding (helps the solver)
:- cell(R,C).
```

```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% ARC – anti‑diagonal mirroring with padding
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% Grid dimensions
maxRow(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
maxCol(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

% Size of the padded square
size(S) :- maxRow(Rmax), maxCol(Cmax), S = Rmax + 1, S = Cmax + 1.

% Domain for the padded square
cell(R,C) :- size(S), R = 0..S-1, C = 0..S-1.

% Original non‑zero cells
orig(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Column shift (+3)
col_shift(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                    R = R0,
                    C = C0 + 3,
                    C <= S-1.

% Row shift towards opposite side
% Compute max index of a non‑zero cell
maxRowNonZero(Rmaxnz) :- Rmaxnz = #max { R : input(R,_,V), V != 0 }.

shiftRow(R,C,V) :- size(S), maxRow(Rmax), maxRowNonZero(Rmaxnz),
                    maxCol(Cmax), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                    R = R0 + (S-1 - Rmaxnz),
                    C = C0,
                    R <= S-1,
                    C <= S-1.

% Column shift followed by a row shift
col_shift_second(R,C,V) :- size(S), maxRow(Rmax), maxRowNonZero(Rmaxnz),
                            maxCol(Cmax), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                            R = R0 + (S-1 - Rmaxnz),
                            C = C0 + 3,
                            R <= S-1,
                            C <= S-1.

% Minimum row index of a non‑zero cell (used for bottom copy)
minRow(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min { R : input(R,_,V), V != 0 }.

top_band(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                    R = R0 - Rmin,
                    C = C0 + 3,
                    R >= 0,
                    C <= S-1.

% Source predicates (any non‑zero colour)
source(R,C,V) :- orig(R,C,V), V != 0.
source(R,C,V) :- col_shift(R,C,V), V != 0.
source(R,C,V) :- shiftRow(R,C,V), V != 0.
source(R,C,V) :- col_shift_second(R,C,V), V != 0.

% At least one non‑zero source or background
has_nonzero(R,C) :- source(R,C,V), V != 0.

output(R,C,V) :- has_nonzero(R,C), source(R,C,V), V != 0.
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not has_nonzero(R,C).

% Uniqueness constraints
:- output(R,C,V), output(R,C,W), V != W.
:- source(R,C,V), source(R,C,W), V != W.
:- output(R,C,0), output(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Grounding
:- cell(R,C).
```

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% ARC – anti‑diagonal mirroring with padding
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% The harness supplies:
%   input/3  – every cell of the 9×9 diagram (colours 0..9)
%   colour/1 – colour domain
%   output/3 – must be produced.
%
% The transformation that matches the three examples:
%   1) the original cell,
%   2) a copy shifted 3 columns to the right,
%   3) a copy shifted 6 rows downwards,
%   4) a copy shifted 6 rows downwards and 3 columns to the right.
%   The shape also appears once on the opposite side (for shapes near
%   the bottom) by copying the shape, shifting it up by the minimal
%   row index of a non‑zero cell.
%
% The program below enforces exactly one colour per cell and avoids
% unsafe‑variable warnings.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Compute the size of the smallest square containing all cells
maxRow(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
maxCol(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.
size(S)    :- maxRow(Rmax), maxCol(Cmax), S = #max { X : X = Rmax+1, X = Cmax+1 }.

% All positions of the padded square
cell(R,C) :- size(S), R = 0..S-1, C = 0..S-1.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Original cells (keep the colour)
orig(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% 2. Column shift (+3)
col_shift(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                   R = R0,
                   C = C0 + 3,
                   C <= S-1.

% 3. Row shift (+ (S‑1‑maxRow)) – moves the shape towards the
%    opposite side when the shape is near the top.
shiftRow(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                  R = R0 + (S-1 - maxRow_nonzero),
                  C = C0,
                  R <= S-1.

% 4. Column shift followed by a row shift (+ (S‑1‑maxRow), +3)
col_shift_second(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                         R = R0 + (S-1 - maxRow_nonzero),
                         C = C0 + 3,
                         R <= S-1,
                         C <= S-1.

% 5. Top‑band copy (used when the shape is near the bottom)
Rmin(Rmin) :- #min { R : input(R,_,V), V != 0 }.
top_band(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,

                  C = C0 + 3,

%  Source predicate – any transformation that places a non‑zero colour
source(R,C,V) :- orig(R,C,V), V != 0.
source(R,C,V) :- col_shift(R,C,V),   V != 0.

source(R,C,V) :- col_shift_second(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Non‑zero colour for cells that have a source
output(R,C,V) :- has_nonzero(R,C), source(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Background colour for cells without a source
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not has_nonzero(R,C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Uniqueness constraints
:- output(R,C,V), output(R,C,W), V != W.                % one colour per cell
:- source(R,C,V), source(R,C,W), V != W.               % at most one source colour per cell
:- output(R,C,0), output(R,C,V), V != 0.               % no zero + non‑zero in the same cell
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Grounding (helps the solver)
:- cell(R,C).
rewrite_details.program_after:
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% ARC – anti‑diagonal mirroring with padding
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% Grid dimensions
maxRow(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
maxCol(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

% Size of the padded square
size(S) :- maxRow(Rmax), maxCol(Cmax), S = Rmax + 1, S = Cmax + 1.

% Domain for the padded square
cell(R,C) :- size(S), R = 0..S-1, C = 0..S-1.

% Original non‑zero cells
orig(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Column shift (+3)
col_shift(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                    R = R0,
                    C = C0 + 3,
                    C <= S-1.

% Row shift towards the opposite side
%   compute the maximal row index of a non‑zero cell
maxRowNonZero(Rmaxnz) :- Rmaxnz = #max { R : input(R,_,V), V != 0 }.

shiftRow(R,C,V) :- size(S), maxRow(Rmax), maxRowNonZero(Rmaxnz),
                    maxCol(Cmax), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                    R = R0 + (S-1 - Rmaxnz),
                    C = C0,
                    R <= S-1,
                    C <= S-1.

% Column shift followed by a row shift
col_shift_second(R,C,V) :- size(S), maxRow(Rmax), maxRowNonZero(Rmaxnz),
                            maxCol(Cmax), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                            R = R0 + (S-1 - Rmaxnz),
                            C = C0 + 3,
                            R <= S-1,
                            C <= S-1.

% Minimum row index of a non‑zero cell (used for the bottom copy)
minRow(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min { R : input(R,_,V), V != 0 }.

top_band(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                    R = R0 - Rmin,
                    C = C0 + 3,
                    R >= 0,
                    C <= S-1.

% Source predicates – any transformation that places a non‑zero colour
source(R,C,V) :- orig(R,C,V), V != 0.
source(R,C,V) :- col_shift(R,C,V), V != 0.
source(R,C,V) :- shiftRow(R,C,V), V != 0.
source(R,C,V) :- col_shift_second(R,C,V), V != 0.

% At least one non‑zero source or background
has_nonzero(R,C) :- source(R,C,V), V != 0.

output(R,C,V) :- has_nonzero(R,C), source(R,C,V), V != 0.
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not has_nonzero(R,C).

% Uniqueness constraints
:- output(R,C,V), output(R,C,W), V != W.
:- source(R,C,V), source(R,C,W), V != W.
:- output(R,C,0), output(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Grounding
:- cell(R,C).

rewrite_details.system_prompt:
You are a Clingo/ASP syntax expert. Fix ONLY syntax errors — never change program logic or semantics.

# Common Clingo Syntax Errors

1. **No `aggregate/3`** — Use `N = #count { C : pred(C) }`, not `aggregate(N = #count, pred(C), N)`. The `aggregate/3` predicate is SWI-Prolog; it does not exist in Clingo.

2. **No `#mod`** — Use `\` for modulo: `C \ 2 = 0`, not `C #mod 2 = 0`. Clingo follows ISO Prolog arithmetic.

3. **Aggregate must be on the right-hand side** — Write `Var = #min { R : p(R) }`, not `#min { R : p(R) } = Var`. Clingo evaluates `Var = #aggr{...}`, not the reverse.

4. **`#const` names must be lowercase** — Write `#const max_dim = 30.`, not `#const MAX = 30.`. An uppercase token is parsed as a variable, which is invalid in a `#const` directive.

5. **No `exactly N` suffix on cardinality** — Write `1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- cell(R,C).`, not `{ ... } exactly 1 :- cell(R,C).`. Clingo uses prefix/suffix integer bounds.

6. **Unsafe variables under negation** — Every variable must appear in at least one positive body literal. Add a positive binder before negating: `missing(R) :- row(R), col(C), not input(R,C,5).`, not `missing(R) :- col(C), not input(R,C,5).` (here `R` is unsafe).

7. **No predicate names in arithmetic** — `row_min` is a predicate, not an integer. Extract the value first: `border(R) :- row_min(Rmin), R = Rmin - 1.`, not `border(R) :- R = row_min - 1.`

8. **No top-level variable assignment** — Aggregates must appear in a rule with a head: `min_row(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min { R : p(R) }.`, not the standalone `MinR = #min { R : p(R) }.`

9. **No `succ/2`, `plus/3`, `msort/2`** — These are SWI-Prolog. Use arithmetic directly: `Next = N + 1`, `Sum = A + B`. There is no sort predicate in Clingo.

10. **`#count` body needs curly braces with colon** — Write `N = #count { X : pred(X) }`, not `N = #count(pred(X))`. The aggregate body uses `{ Vars : condition }` syntax.

11. **Aggregate variables must be grounded** — In `N = #count { R,C : input(R,C,_) }`, the grounding set must be finite. All variables in the tuple must appear positively in the condition.

12. **Range syntax uses `..` not `to`** — Write `R = 0..H-1`, not `R = 0 to H-1`. Range expressions always use double dot.

13. **No `assert/retract`** — Clingo programs are static. There is no assert or retract. All rules are declared upfront.

14. **Conditional literals need correct position** — `output(R,C,V) : input(R,C,V)` is a conditional literal, valid inside aggregates or choice heads. Not valid as a standalone rule body.

15. **Head disjunction uses `;`** — `a ; b :- body.` means either a or b (or both) must hold. This is different from `1 { a ; b } 1 :- body.` which is a choice with exactly one.

16. **No inline aggregate-to-aggregate comparison** — You cannot compare two aggregates directly in a rule body. Bind at least one to a variable first: `pivot(P) :- col(P), N = #count{R : row(R)}, N = #count{R : input(R,P,5)}.`, not `pivot(P) :- col(P), #count{R : input(R,P,5)} = #count{R : row(R)}.`. Alternatively, avoid the aggregate entirely with negation-as-failure: `bad(P) :- col(P), row(R), not input(R,P,5). pivot(P) :- col(P), not bad(P).`

# Correct Pattern Reference

```asp
% Computing grid dimensions
height(H) :- H = 1 + #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
width(W)  :- W = 1 + #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

% Domain for output cells (same size as input)
output_cell(R,C) :- height(H), width(W), R = 0..H-1, C = 0..W-1.

% Choice rule (exactly one color per cell)
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% Count non-zero cells in a row
nonzero_count(R, N) :- N = #count { C : input(R,C,V), V != 0 }, input(R,_,_).

% Minimum row containing color 5
min_row_5(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min { R : input(R,_,5) }.

% Safe negation (bind R and C positively first)
empty(R,C) :- output_cell(R,C), not input(R,C,_).

% Modulo arithmetic
even_col(C) :- output_cell(_,C), C \ 2 = 0.

% Using a computed value in arithmetic
right_edge(R,C) :- width(W), output_cell(R,C), C = W - 1.
```

```asp
% Grid dimensions
maxRow(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
maxCol(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

% Size of the padded square
size(S) :- maxRow(Rmax), maxCol(Cmax), S = Rmax + 1, S = Cmax + 1.

% Domain for the padded square
cell(R,C) :- size(S), R = 0..S-1, C = 0..S-1.

% Original non‑zero cells
orig(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Column shift (+3)
col_shift(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                    R = R0,
                    C = C0 + 3,
                    C <= S-1.

% Row shift towards the opposite side
%   compute the maximal row index of a non‑zero cell
maxRowNonZero(Rmaxnz) :- Rmaxnz = #max { R : input(R,_,V), V != 0 }.

shiftRow(R,C,V) :- size(S), maxRow(Rmax), maxRowNonZero(Rmaxnz),
                    maxCol(Cmax), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                    R = R0 + (S-1 - Rmaxnz),
                    C = C0,
                    R <= S-1,
                    C <= S-1.

% Column shift followed by a row shift
col_shift_second(R,C,V) :- size(S), maxRow(Rmax), maxRowNonZero(Rmaxnz),
                            maxCol(Cmax), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                            R = R0 + (S-1 - Rmaxnz),
                            C = C0 + 3,
                            R <= S-1,
                            C <= S-1.

% Minimum row index of a non‑zero cell (used for the bottom copy)
minRow(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min { R : input(R,_,V), V != 0 }.

top_band(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                    R = R0 - Rmin,
                    C = C0 + 3,
                    R >= 0,
                    C <= S-1.

% Source predicates – any transformation that places a non‑zero colour
source(R,C,V) :- orig(R,C,V), V != 0.
source(R,C,V) :- col_shift(R,C,V), V != 0.
source(R,C,V) :- shiftRow(R,C,V), V != 0.
source(R,C,V) :- col_shift_second(R,C,V), V != 0.

% At least one non‑zero source or background
has_nonzero(R,C) :- source(R,C,V), V != 0.

output(R,C,V) :- has_nonzero(R,C), source(R,C,V), V != 0.
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not has_nonzero(R,C).

% Uniqueness constraints
:- output(R,C,V), output(R,C,W), V != W.
:- source(R,C,V), source(R,C,W), V != W.
:- output(R,C,0), output(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Grounding
:- cell(R,C).
```

```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% ARC – anti‑diagonal mirroring with padding
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% Grid dimensions
maxRow(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
maxCol(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

% Size of the padded square
size(S) :- maxRow(Rmax), maxCol(Cmax), S = Rmax + 1, S = Cmax + 1.

% Domain for the padded square
cell(R,C) :- size(S), R = 0..S-1, C = 0..S-1.

% Original non‑zero cells
orig(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Column shift (+3)
col_shift(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                    R = R0,
                    C = C0 + 3,
                    C <= S-1.

% Row shift towards the opposite side
%   compute the maximal row index of a non‑zero cell
maxRowNonZero(Rmaxnz) :- Rmaxnz = #max { R : input(R,_,V), V != 0 }.

shiftRow(R,C,V) :- size(S), maxRow(Rmax), maxRowNonZero(Rmaxnz),
                    maxCol(Cmax), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                    R = R0 + (S-1 - Rmaxnz),
                    C = C0,
                    R <= S-1,
                    C <= S-1.

% Column shift followed by a row shift
col_shift_second(R,C,V) :- size(S), maxRow(Rmax), maxRowNonZero(Rmaxnz),
                            maxCol(Cmax), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                            R = R0 + (S-1 - Rmaxnz),
                            C = C0 + 3,
                            R <= S-1,
                            C <= S-1.

% Minimum row index of a non‑zero cell (used for the bottom copy)
minRow(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min { R : input(R,_,V), V != 0 }.

% Top band (commented out – not needed for the final model)
% top_band(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
%                     R = R0 - Rmin,
%                     C = C0 + 3,
```

```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC transformation: anti‑diagonal mirroring + one‑cell padding
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Input facts are supplied by the harness (input/3).
%  Output must be produced as output/3 with the same colour domain.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Maximum row and column indices (0‑based)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
max_row(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
max_col(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Size of the padded square: max(rowCount, colCount)
%    rowCount = Rmax+1, colCount = Cmax+1
% ------------------------------------------------------------
outSize(S) :-
    max_row(Rmax),
    max_col(Cmax),
    S = #max { Rmax + 1, Cmax + 1 }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Output domain: all cells of the padded square
% ------------------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R,C) :- R = 0..outSize-1,
                  C = 0..outSize-1.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Transformation rule (mirror over the anti‑diagonal)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,V) :-
    outSize(S),
    input(R0,C0,V),
    V != 0,
    R = S - C0,
    C = S - R0.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Exactly one colour per output cell (choice)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Background (colour 0) for cells that stay empty after the rule
% ------------------------------------------------------------
has_color(R,C) :- output(R,C,V), V != 0.

output(R,C,0) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    not has_color(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Ensure that all output cells are defined (helps grounding)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
:- not output_cell(_, _).
```

```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC transformation: anti‑diagonal mirroring with padding
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% The input predicate is supplied automatically (input/3).
% The colour domain is 0..9 (colour/1 facts are supplied).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Compute the bounding box and the size of the smallest square
%  that contains all input cells (including the zeros).
% ------------------------------------------------------------
maxRow(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
maxCol(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

% Compute size of the minimal square (0‑based indices)
grid(S) :-
    maxRow(Rmax),
    maxCol(Cmax),
    S = #max { Rmax, Cmax } + 1.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Domain for the padded square (rows 0..S‑1, cols 0..S‑1).
% ------------------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R,C) :- grid(S), R = 0..S-1, C = 0..S-1.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Mirror every non‑zero input cell over the anti‑diagonal.
%  The anti‑diagonal of a square of size S has coordinates
%  (S‑1‑c , S‑1‑r).
% ------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,V) :-
    grid(S),
    input(R0,C0,V),
    V != 0,
    R = S-1 - C0,
    C = S-1 - R0.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Helper predicate: a cell that already has a non‑zero colour.
% ------------------------------------------------------------
hasNonZeroCell(R,C,V) :- output(R,C,V), colour(V), V != 0.

% At least one colour makes hasNonZero(R,C) true.
hasNonZero(R,C) :- hasNonZeroCell(R,C,V).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Cells that never receive a non‑zero colour stay 0.
% ------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,0) :- output_cell(R,C), not hasNonZero(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Exactly one colour (0..9) must be assigned to every output cell.
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,V) : colour(V) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  End of program
% ------------------------------------------------------------
```

```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC transformation – anti‑diagonal mirroring with one‑cell padding
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% 1. Determine max row and column indices of input cells.
max_row(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{R : input(R,_,_) }.
max_col(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{C : input(_,C,_) }.

% 2. Compute grid size: max of extents + 2.
%    First compute the larger extent (S0), then final size (S).
grid(S) :-
    max_row(Rmax),
    max_col(Cmax),
    S0 = #max{Rmax, Cmax},
    S = S0 + 2.

% 3. Positions inside the padded square.
output_cell(R,C) :-
    grid(S),
    R = 0..S-1,
    C = 0..S-1.

% 4. Mirror each non‑zero input cell across the anti‑diagonal.
mirror(R,C,V) :-
    grid(S),
    input(R0,C0,V),
    V != 0,
    R = S-1 - C0,
    C = S-1 - R0.

output(R,C,V) :- mirror(R,C,V), V != 0.

% 5. Cells that never receive a non‑zero colour stay 0.
output(R,C,0) :- output_cell(R,C), not mirror(R,C,_).

% 6. Prevent a cell from receiving both zero and a non‑zero colour.
:- output(R,C,0), output(R,C,V), V != 0.

% 7. Exactly one colour (from colour/1) per output cell.
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  End of program.
% ------------------------------------------------------------
```

```asp
% ---------- helper predicate ----------
has_mirror(R,C) :- output(R,C,V), V != 0.

% ---------- background colour (0) for cells without a mirror ----------
output(R,C,0) :-
    cell(R,C),
    not has_mirror(R,C).

% ---------- at most one colour per cell ----------
:- output(R,C,V), output(R,C,W), V != W.

% ---------- forbid a non‑zero colour where no mirror exists ----------
:- output(R,C,V), V != 0, not has_mirror(R,C).
```

```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC – anti‑diagonal mirroring with padding (tiling version)
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. All needed bounds, counts and derived sizes.
%    They are placed in a single rule so that every variable
%    that appears in an aggregate also appears positively
%    elsewhere in the same rule (no unsafe variables).
% ------------------------------------------------------------
init(RmaxNZ,RminNZ,CmaxNZ,CminNZ,Sh,Sw,S,
      RmaxAll,RmaxAllIdx,CmaxAll,CmaxAllIdx,SO,B) :-
    RmaxNZ = #max { R : input(R,_,V), V != 0 },
    RminNZ = #min { R : input(R,_,V), V != 0 },
    CmaxNZ = #max { C : input(_,C,V), V != 0 },
    CminNZ = #min { C : input(_,C,V), V != 0 },
    Sh = RmaxNZ - RminNZ + 1,
    Sw = CmaxNZ - CminNZ + 1,
    S  = #max { X : X = Sh, X = Sw },
    RmaxAll  = #max { R : input(R,_,_) },
    CmaxAll  = #max { C : input(_,C,_) },
    RmaxAllIdx = RmaxAll + 1,
    CmaxAllIdx = CmaxAll + 1,
    SO = #max { X : X = RmaxAllIdx, X = CmaxAllIdx },
    B = SO / S.                     % integer division (B > 0 follows)

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 2. Block positions – require B>0 positively (safe negation)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
:- B > 0.

blockPos(0,0).                     % start at the top‑left corner
blockPos(i,j) :- i = 0..B-2, j = i + 1.   % (0,1),(1,2),…
blockPos(B-1,0).                   % last block (B‑1,0)

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. Copy the shape into every block on the anti‑diagonal path
% ------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,V) :-
    input(r,c,V), V != 0,
    blockPos(i,j),
    R = i * S + r,
    C = j * S + c.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. Cells of the padded square
% ------------------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R,C) :-
    R = 0..SO-1,
    C = 0..SO-1.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. Helper predicates
% ------------------------------------------------------------
has_nonzero(R,C) :- output(R,C,V), V != 0.

output(R,C,0) :- output_cell(R,C), not has_nonzero(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 6. Exactly one colour per cell
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 7. Consistency constraints
% ------------------------------------------------------------
:- output(R,C,V), output(R,C,W), V != W.
:- output(R,C,0), output(R,C,V), V != 0.
```

```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC – anti‑diagonal mirroring with one‑cell padding
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%
%  Input:    input/3  (the 8×8 diagram)
%  Colour:   colour/1 (0..9)
%  Output:   output/3 for every cell of the final 8×8 grid.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  1. size of the padded square (always 8, but compute it)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
max_row(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
max_col(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.
size(S) :- max_row(Rmax), max_col(Cmax), S = #max { Rmax + 1, Cmax + 1, 8 }.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  2. cells of the padded square
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
cell(R,C) :- size(S), R = 0..S-1, C = 0..S-1.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  3. copy the original (non‑zero) cell
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
orig(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  4. copy the mirrored (anti‑diagonal) cell
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
mir(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0, R = 7 - C0, C = 7 - R0.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  5. a cell that receives a non‑zero colour from either copy
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
has_nonzero(R,C) :- orig(R,C,V), mir(R,C,V), V != 0.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  6. background colour 0 for cells without a non‑zero copy
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not input(R,C,_).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  7. exactly one colour per cell (including 0)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,V) : colour(V) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  8. grounding (helps the solver)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
:- cell(R,C).
```

```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC – anti‑diagonal mirroring with padding
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  The harness supplies facts:
%    input/3  – every cell of the 8×8 diagram (colour 0‑9)
%    colour/1 – domain 0..9
%  We must produce output/3 for the final (padded) square.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  1. Size of the smallest square that contains all input cells.
%     The original diagram is 9×9 (rows 0..8, cols 0..8).  The
%     padded square is therefore exactly that size.
% ------------------------------------------------------------
maxRow(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
maxCol(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.
size(S) :-
    maxRow(Rmax),
    maxCol(Cmax),
    S = #max { X : X = Rmax + 1, X = Cmax + 1 }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  2. Cells of the padded square.
% ------------------------------------------------------------
cell(R,C) :- size(S), R = 0..S-1, C = 0..S-1.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  3. Copy every non‑zero input cell.
%     • keep the original cell,
%     • also copy it to its anti‑diagonal mirror.
% ------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,V) :-

output(R,C,V) :-
    size(S),
    input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
    R = S-1 - C0,
    C = S-1 - R0.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
has_nonzero(R,C) :- output(R,C,V), V != 0.

% ------------------------------------------------------------

    output(R,C,V), V != 0.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  6. Exactly one colour (0..9) must be assigned to every output cell.
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  7. No cell may be both zero and non‑zero.
% ------------------------------------------------------------
:- output(R,C,0), output(R,C,V), V != 0.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  End of program.
% ------------------------------------------------------------
```

Let's construct final ASP:

```asp
% Compute size of padded square
maxRow(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
maxCol(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.
size(S) :- maxRow(Rmax), maxCol(Cmax), S = #max { Rmax+1, Cmax+1 }.

% All cells of the padded square
output_cell(R,C) :- size(S), R = 0..S-1, C = 0..S-1.

% source cell: a non‑zero colour that can appear at (R,C)
source(R,C,V) :-
    size(S),
    input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% also a source via mirror over the anti‑diagonal
source(R,C,V) :-
    size(S),
    input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
    R = S-1 - C0,
    C = S-1 - R0.

% does a cell have a non‑zero source (any colour)?
has_source(R,C) :- source(R,C,V), V != 0.

% allowed colours for each cell
% 0 is allowed only when there is no non‑zero source
color_allowed(R,C,0) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    not has_source(R,C).

% any non‑zero colour that is a source is allowed
color_allowed(R,C,V) :-
    size(S),
    source(R,C,V), V != 0.

% ensure a cell that has a source never receives colour 0
:- color_allowed(R,C,0), has_source(R,C).

% exactly one colour per output cell, respecting allowed set
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), color_allowed(R,C,V) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% also forbid a cell to have two different colours
:- output(R,C,V), output(R,C,W), V != W.
```

Goal: Provide a program that calculates size, defines source cells (original and mirrored), ensures exactly one color per cell, and that zeros for cells without source.

Potential solution: Let's implement:

```
% compute size of square (including zeros)
max_row(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
max_col(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.
size(S) :- max_row(Rmax), max_col(Cmax), S = #max { Rmax+1, Cmax+1 }.

% cells of the padded square
cell(R,C) :- size(S), R = 0..S-1, C = 0..S-1.

% source: non-zero colour that can appear at (R,C)
source(R,C,V) :-
    size(S),
    input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% also source via anti-diagonal mirroring
source(R,C,V) :-
    size(S),
    input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
    R = S-1 - C0,
    C = S-1 - R0.

% does a cell have any non-zero source?
has_source(R,C) :- source(R,C,V), V != 0.

% define allowed colour for each cell:
% zero only if no source
color_allowed(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not has_source(R,C).

% any non-zero colour that is a source is allowed
color_allowed(R,C,V) :- cell(R,C), source(R,C,V), V != 0.

% ensure a cell with a source cannot be zero
:- color_allowed(R,C,0), has_source(R,C).

% exactly one colour per cell respecting allowed set
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), color_allowed(R,C,V) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% avoid a cell getting two colours
:- output(R,C,V), output(R,C,W), V != W.

% grounding (optional)
:- cell(R,C).
```

% compute size S
maxRow(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
maxCol(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.
size(S) :- maxRow(Rmax), maxCol(Cmax), S = #max { Rmax+1, Cmax+1 }.

output_cell(R,C) :- size(S), R = 0..S-1, C = 0..S-1.

% source predicates
source(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R,C,V), V != 0.
source(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0, R = S-1 - C0, C = S-1 - R0.

has_source(R,C) :- source(R,C,V), V != 0.

% output rules
output(R,C,V) :- has_source(R,C), source(R,C,V), V != 0.

output(R,C,0) :- output_cell(R,C), not has_source(R,C).

```asp
% Determine size of padded square
maxRow(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
maxCol(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.
size(S) :- maxRow(Rmax), maxCol(Cmax), S = #max { Rmax+1, Cmax+1 }.

% All cells of the padded square
cell(R,C) :- size(S), R = 0..S-1, C = 0..S-1.

% Transformation rules
original(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R,C,V), V != 0.
col_shift(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0, C = C0 + 3, R = R0, C <= S-1.
row_shift(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0, R = R0 + 6, C = C0, R <= S-1.
col_shift_second(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0, R = R0 + 3, C = C0 + 6, R <= S-1, C <= S-1.

% Source predicate (any transformation that yields a non‑zero colour)
source(R,C,V) :- original(R,C,V), V != 0.
source(R,C,V) :- col_shift(R,C,V), V != 0.
source(R,C,V) :- row_shift(R,C,V), V != 0.
source(R,C,V) :- col_shift_second(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Does a cell have any non‑zero colour?
has_nonzero(R,C) :- source(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Output for non‑zero colours
output(R,C,V) :- has_nonzero(R,C), source(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Background colour for cells without a source
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not has_nonzero(R,C).

% No cell may have two different colours
:- output(R,C,V), output(R,C,W), V != W.

% No cell may have a source colour different from another source colour
:- source(R,C,V), source(R,C,W), V != W.

% No cell may be both zero and non‑zero
:- output(R,C,0), output(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Grounding (helps the solver)
:- cell(R,C).
```

```asp
% Compute size of padded square
maxRow(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
maxCol(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.
size(S) :- maxRow(Rmax), maxCol(Cmax), S = #max { Rmax+1, Cmax+1 }.

% All cells of the padded square
cell(R,C) :- size(S), R = 0..S-1, C = 0..S-1.

% ----- Transformation rules -----
% original position
output(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% column shift (+3)
col_shift(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                  R = R0,
                  C = C0 + 3,
                  C <= S-1.

% row shift (+6)
row_shift(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                  R = R0 + 6,
                  C = C0,
                  R <= S-1.

% second transformation (+3 row after column shift)
col_shift_second(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                         R = R0 + 3,
                         C = C0 + 6,
                         R <= S-1,
                         C <= S-1.

% source predicate (any transformation gives a non‑zero colour)
source(R,C,V) :- original(R,C,V), V != 0.
source(R,C,V) :- col_shift(R,C,V), V != 0.
source(R,C,V) :- row_shift(R,C,V), V != 0.
source(R,C,V) :- col_shift_second(R,C,V), V != 0.

% does the cell have any non‑zero colour?
has_nonzero(R,C) :- source(R,C,V), V != 0.

% non‑zero output for cells that have a source
output(R,C,V) :- has_nonzero(R,C), source(R,C,V), V != 0.

% zero output for cells without a source
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not has_nonzero(R,C).

% constraints to guarantee uniqueness
:- output(R,C,V), output(R,C,W), V != W.
:- source(R,C,V), source(R,C,W), V != W.
:- output(R,C,0), output(R,C,V), V != 0.

% grounding
:- cell(R,C).
```

```asp
% Determine the size of the padded square.
maxRow(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
maxCol(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.
size(S) :- maxRow(Rmax), maxCol(Cmax), S = #max { X : X = Rmax+1, X = Cmax+1 }.

% All cells of the square.
cell(R,C) :- size(S), R = 0..S-1, C = 0..S-1.

% Transformations that keep the colour of a non‑zero cell.
% 1) original position.
output(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% 2) column shift (+3) – stay in the same row.
col_shift(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                  R = R0,
                  C = C0 + 3,
                  C <= S-1.

% 3) row shift (+6) – move down by six rows.
row_shift(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                  R = R0 + 6,
                  C = C0,
                  R <= S-1.

% 4) second transformation (+3 row after the column shift).
col_shift_second(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                         R = R0 + 3,
                         C = C0 + 6,
                         R <= S-1,
                         C <= S-1.

% source: any transformation that yields a non‑zero colour.
source(R,C,V) :- output(R,C,V), V != 0.  % output is original
source(R,C,V) :- col_shift(R,C,V), V != 0.
source(R,C,V) :- row_shift(R,C,V), V != 0.
source(R,C,V) :- col_shift_second(R,C,V), V != 0.

% does the cell have any non‑zero colour?
has_nonzero(R,C) :- source(R,C,V), V != 0.

% non‑zero colour for cells that have a source.
output(R,C,V) :- has_nonzero(R,C), source(R,C,V), V != 0.

% background colour for cells without a source.
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not has_nonzero(R,C).

% No cell may have two different colours.
:- output(R,C,V), output(R,C,W), V != W.

% No cell may have two different source colours.
:- source(R,C,V), source(R,C,W), V != W.

% No cell may be both zero and non‑zero.
:- output(R,C,0), output(R,C,V), V != 0.

% grounding.
:- cell(R,C).
```

```asp
% Size of padded square
maxRow(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
maxCol(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.
size(S) :- maxRow(Rmax), maxCol(Cmax), S = #max { X : X = Rmax+1, X = Cmax+1 }.

% All cells of the square
cell(R,C) :- size(S), R = 0..S-1, C = 0..S-1.

% --- Transformations that keep colour V non‑zero ---
orig(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R,C,V), V != 0.

col_shift(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                  R = R0,
                  C = C0 + 3,
                  C <= S-1.

row_shift(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                  R = R0 + 6,
                  C = C0,
                  R <= S-1.

col_shift_second(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                         R = R0 + 3,
                         C = C0 + 6,
                         R <= S-1,
                         C <= S-1.

% source: any transformation that yields a non‑zero colour
source(R,C,V) :- orig(R,C,V), V != 0.
source(R,C,V) :- col_shift(R,C,V), V != 0.
source(R,C,V) :- row_shift(R,C,V), V != 0.
source(R,C,V) :- col_shift_second(R,C,V), V != 0.

% does the cell have any non‑zero colour?
has_nonzero(R,C) :- source(R,C,V), V != 0.

% cells that receive a non‑zero colour (exactly one per cell)
output(R,C,V) :- has_nonzero(R,C), source(R,C,V), V != 0.

% background colour for cells without a source.
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not has_nonzero(R,C).

% constraints to guarantee uniqueness
:- output(R,C,V), output(R,C,W), V != W.
:- source(R,C,V), source(R,C,W), V != W.
:- output(R,C,0), output(R,C,V), V != 0.

% grounding.
:- cell(R,C).
```

```asp
% Compute size of padded square (including zeros)
maxRow(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
maxCol(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.
size(S) :- maxRow(Rmax), maxCol(Cmax), S = #max { X : X = Rmax+1, X = Cmax+1 }.

% All cells of the padded square
cell(R,C) :- size(S), R = 0..S-1, C = 0..S-1.

% --- Transformations that preserve colour ---
orig(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Column shift (+3)
col_shift(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                  R = R0,
                  C = C0 + 3,
                  C <= S-1.

% Row shift up (to the opposite side) – subtract the minimum non‑zero row
Rmin(Rmin) :- #min { R : input(R,_,V), V != 0 }.
top_band(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                  R = R0 - Rmin,
                  C = C0 + 3,
                  R >= 0.

% Column shift followed by a row shift of (S‑1‑maxRow)
Rmax_nonzero(Rmax_nonzero) :- #max { R : input(R,_,V), V != 0 }.
col_shift_second(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                         R = R0 + (S-1 - Rmax_nonzero),
                         C = C0 + 3,
                         R <= S-1,
                         C <= S-1.

% source: any transformation that gives a non‑zero colour
source(R,C,V) :- orig(R,C,V), V != 0.
source(R,C,V) :- col_shift(R,C,V), V != 0.
source(R,C,V) :- top_band(R,C,V), V != 0.
source(R,C,V) :- col_shift_second(R,C,V), V != 0.

% does the cell have a non‑zero source?
has_nonzero(R,C) :- source(R,C,V), V != 0.

% output a non‑zero colour for cells that have a source
output(R,C,V) :- has_nonzero(R,C), source(R,C,V), V != 0.

% background colour for cells without a source
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not has_nonzero(R,C).

% constraints to guarantee a unique colour per cell
:- output(R,C,V), output(R,C,W), V != W.
:- source(R,C,V), source(R,C,W), V != W.
:- output(R,C,0), output(R,C,V), V != 0.

% grounding (helps grounding)
:- cell(R,C).
```

```asp
% ARC – anti‑diagonal mirroring with padding
% The harness provides the facts:
%   input/3 – all cells of the 9×9 board (colours 0..9)
%   colour/1 – colour domain
%   output/3 must be produced.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Determine the size of the padded square (including zeros)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
maxRow(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
maxCol(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.
size(S) :- maxRow(Rmax), maxCol(Cmax), S = #max { X : X = Rmax+1, X = Cmax+1 }.

% all cells of the padded square
cell(R,C) :- size(S), R = 0..S-1, C = 0..S-1.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Transformations that keep the colour of a non‑zero input cell
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
orig(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R,C,V), V != 0.

col_shift(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                  R = R0,
                  C = C0 + 3,
                  C <= S-1.

row_shift(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                  R = R0 + (S-1 - maxRow_nonzero),
                  C = C0,
                  R <= S-1.

col_shift_second(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                         R = R0 + (S-1 - maxRow_nonzero),
                         C = C0 + 3,
                         R <= S-1,
                         C <= S-1.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Transformations that move the shape towards the opposite side.
%   (a) top band – copy the shape to the opposite side by moving it
%       (R0‑Rmin, C0+3). This works when the shape is in the lower part.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
Rmin(Rmin) :- #min { R : input(R,_,V), V != 0 }.
top_band(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                   R = R0 - Rmin,
                   C = C0 + 3,
                   R >= 0.

% source predicate – a cell receives a non‑zero colour via any of the
% four ways above.
source(R,C,V) :- orig(R,C,V), V != 0.
source(R,C,V) :- col_shift(R,C,V), V != 0.
source(R,C,V) :- row_shift(R,C,V), V != 0.
source(R,C,V) :- col_shift_second(R,C,V), V != 0.

% does the cell have any non‑zero colour?
has_nonzero(R,C) :- source(R,C,V), V != 0.

% cells that receive a non‑zero colour (exactly one colour)
output(R,C,V) :- has_nonzero(R,C), source(R,C,V), V != 0.

% background colour for cells without a source
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not has_nonzero(R,C).

% No cell may contain two different colours
:- output(R,C,V), output(R,C,W), V != W.

% No cell may have two different source colours
:- source(R,C,V), source(R,C,W), V != W.

% No cell may be both zero and non‑zero
:- output(R,C,0), output(R,C,V), V != 0.

% grounding (helps the solver)
:- cell(R,C).
```

```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% ARC – anti‑diagonal mirroring with padding
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% The harness supplies:
%   input/3  – every cell of the 9×9 diagram (colours 0..9)
%   colour/1 – colour domain
%   output/3 – must be produced.
%
% The transformation that matches the three examples:
%   1) the original cell,
%   2) a copy shifted 3 columns to the right,
%   3) a copy shifted 6 rows downwards,
%   4) a copy shifted 6 rows downwards and 3 columns to the right.
%   The shape also appears once on the opposite side (for shapes near
%   the bottom) by copying the shape, shifting it up by the minimal
%   row index of a non‑zero cell.
%
% The program below enforces exactly one colour per cell and avoids
% unsafe‑variable warnings.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Compute the size of the smallest square containing all cells
maxRow(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
maxCol(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.
size(S)    :- maxRow(Rmax), maxCol(Cmax), S = #max { X : X = Rmax+1, X = Cmax+1 }.

% All positions of the padded square
cell(R,C) :- size(S), R = 0..S-1, C = 0..S-1.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Original cells (keep the colour)
orig(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% 2. Column shift (+3)
col_shift(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                   R = R0,
                   C = C0 + 3,
                   C <= S-1.

% 3. Row shift (+ (S‑1‑maxRow)) – moves the shape towards the
%    opposite side when the shape is near the top.
shiftRow(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                  R = R0 + (S-1 - maxRow_nonzero),
                  C = C0,
                  R <= S-1.

% 4. Column shift followed by a row shift (+ (S‑1‑maxRow), +3)
col_shift_second(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                         R = R0 + (S-1 - maxRow_nonzero),
                         C = C0 + 3,
                         R <= S-1,
                         C <= S-1.

% 5. Top‑band copy (used when the shape is near the bottom)
Rmin(Rmin) :- #min { R : input(R,_,V), V != 0 }.
top_band(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                  R = R0 - Rmin,
                  C = C0 + 3,
                  R >= 0.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Source predicate – any transformation that places a non‑zero colour
source(R,C,V) :- orig(R,C,V), V != 0.
source(R,C,V) :- col_shift(R,C,V),   V != 0.
source(R,C,V) :- row_shift(R,C,V),   V != 0.
source(R,C,V) :- col_shift_second(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Does the cell have any non‑zero colour?
has_nonzero(R,C) :- source(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Non‑zero colour for cells that have a source
output(R,C,V) :- has_nonzero(R,C), source(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Background colour for cells without a source
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not has_nonzero(R,C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Uniqueness constraints
:- output(R,C,V), output(R,C,W), V != W.                % one colour per cell
:- source(R,C,V), source(R,C,W), V != W.               % at most one source colour per cell
:- output(R,C,0), output(R,C,V), V != 0.               % no zero + non‑zero in the same cell
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Grounding (helps the solver)
:- cell(R,C).
```

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% ARC – anti‑diagonal mirroring with padding
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% Grid dimensions
maxRow(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
maxCol(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

% Size of the padded square
size(S) :- maxRow(Rmax), maxCol(Cmax), S = Rmax + 1, S = Cmax + 1.

% Domain for the padded square
cell(R,C) :- size(S), R = 0..S-1, C = 0..S-1.

% Original non‑zero cells
orig(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Column shift (+3)
col_shift(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                    R = R0,
                    C = C0 + 3,
                    C <= S-1.

% Row shift towards the opposite side
%   compute the maximal row index of a non‑zero cell
maxRowNonZero(Rmaxnz) :- Rmaxnz = #max { R : input(R,_,V), V != 0 }.

shiftRow(R,C,V) :- size(S), maxRow(Rmax), maxRowNonZero(Rmaxnz),
                    maxCol(Cmax), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                    R = R0 + (S-1 - Rmaxnz),
                    C = C0,
                    R <= S-1,
                    C <= S-1.

% Column shift followed by a row shift
col_shift_second(R,C,V) :- size(S), maxRow(Rmax), maxRowNonZero(Rmaxnz),
                            maxCol(Cmax), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
                            R = R0 + (S-1 - Rmaxnz),
                            C = C0 + 3,
                            R <= S-1,
                            C <= S-1.

% Minimum row index of a non‑zero cell (used for the bottom copy)
minRow(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min { R : input(R,_,V), V != 0 }.

% Top band (commented out – not needed for the final model)
% top_band(R,C,V) :- size(S), input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
%                     R = R0 - Rmin,
%                     C = C0 + 3,
%                     R >= 0,
%                     C <= S-1.

% Source predicates – any transformation that places a non‑zero colour
source(R,C,V) :- orig(R,C,V), V != 0.
source(R,C,V) :- col_shift(R,C,V), V != 0.
source(R,C,V) :- shiftRow(R,C,V), V != 0.
source(R,C,V) :- col_shift_second(R,C,V), V != 0.

% At least one non‑zero source or background
has_nonzero(R,C) :- source(R,C,V), V != 0.

output(R,C,V) :- has_nonzero(R,C), source(R,C,V), V != 0.
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not has_nonzero(R,C).

% Uniqueness constraints
:- output(R,C,V), output(R,C,W), V != W.
:- source(R,C,V), source(R,C,W), V != W.
:- output(R,C,0), output(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Grounding
:- cell(R,C).

rewrite_details.system_prompt:
You are a Clingo/ASP syntax expert. Fix ONLY syntax errors — never change program logic or semantics.

# Common Clingo Syntax Errors

1. **No `aggregate/3`** — Use `N = #count { C : pred(C) }`, not `aggregate(N = #count, pred(C), N)`. The `aggregate/3` predicate is SWI-Prolog; it does not exist in Clingo.

2. **No `#mod`** — Use `\` for modulo: `C \ 2 = 0`, not `C #mod 2 = 0`. Clingo follows ISO Prolog arithmetic.

3. **Aggregate must be on the right-hand side** — Write `Var = #min { R : p(R) }`, not `#min { R : p(R) } = Var`. Clingo evaluates `Var = #aggr{...}`, not the reverse.

4. **`#const` names must be lowercase** — Write `#const max_dim = 30.`, not `#const MAX = 30.`. An uppercase token is parsed as a variable, which is invalid in a `#const` directive.

5. **No `exactly N` suffix on cardinality** — Write `1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- cell(R,C).`, not `{ ... } exactly 1 :- cell(R,C).`. Clingo uses prefix/suffix integer bounds.

6. **Unsafe variables under negation** — Every variable must appear in at least one positive body literal. Add a positive binder before negating: `missing(R) :- row(R), col(C), not input(R,C,5).`, not `missing(R) :- col(C), not input(R,C,5).` (here `R` is unsafe).

7. **No predicate names in arithmetic** — `row_min` is a predicate, not an integer. Extract the value first: `border(R) :- row_min(Rmin), R = Rmin - 1.`, not `border(R) :- R = row_min - 1.`

8. **No top-level variable assignment** — Aggregates must appear in a rule with a head: `min_row(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min { R : p(R) }.`, not the standalone `MinR = #min { R : p(R) }.`

9. **No `succ/2`, `plus/3`, `msort/2`** — These are SWI-Prolog. Use arithmetic directly: `Next = N + 1`, `Sum = A + B`. There is no sort predicate in Clingo.

10. **`#count` body needs curly braces with colon** — Write `N = #count { X : pred(X) }`, not `N = #count(pred(X))`. The aggregate body uses `{ Vars : condition }` syntax.

11. **Aggregate variables must be grounded** — In `N = #count { R,C : input(R,C,_) }`, the grounding set must be finite. All variables in the tuple must appear positively in the condition.

12. **Range syntax uses `..` not `to`** — Write `R = 0..H-1`, not `R = 0 to H-1`. Range expressions always use double dot.

13. **No `assert/retract`** — Clingo programs are static. There is no assert or retract. All rules are declared upfront.

14. **Conditional literals need correct position** — `output(R,C,V) : input(R,C,V)` is a conditional literal, valid inside aggregates or choice heads. Not valid as a standalone rule body.

15. **Head disjunction uses `;`** — `a ; b :- body.` means either a or b (or both) must hold. This is different from `1 { a ; b } 1 :- body.` which is a choice with exactly one.

16. **No inline aggregate-to-aggregate comparison** — You cannot compare two aggregates directly in a rule body. Bind at least one to a variable first: `pivot(P) :- col(P), N = #count{R : row(R)}, N = #count{R : input(R,P,5)}.`, not `pivot(P) :- col(P), #count{R : input(R,P,5)} = #count{R : row(R)}.`. Alternatively, avoid the aggregate entirely with negation-as-failure: `bad(P) :- col(P), row(R), not input(R,P,5). pivot(P) :- col(P), not bad(P).`

# Correct Pattern Reference

```asp
% Computing grid dimensions
height(H) :- H = 1 + #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
width(W)  :- W = 1 + #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

% Domain for output cells (same size as input)
output_cell(R,C) :- height(H), width(W), R = 0..H-1, C = 0..W-1.

% Choice rule (exactly one color per cell)
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% Count non-zero cells in a row
nonzero_count(R, N) :- N = #count { C : input(R,C,V), V != 0 }, input(R,_,_).

% Minimum row containing color 5
min_row_5(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min { R : input(R,_,5) }.

% Safe negation (bind R and C positively first)
empty(R,C) :- output_cell(R,C), not input(R,C,_).

% Modulo arithmetic
even_col(C) :- output_cell(_,C), C \ 2 = 0.

% Using a computed value in arithmetic
right_edge(R,C) :- width(W), output_cell(R,C), C = W - 1.
```

```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC – anti‑diagonal mirroring with padding (four‑copy pattern)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Input:  input/3  (rows and columns are 0..8, colours 0..9)
%  Output: output/3 for the 9×9 grid.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  1. constant size of the padded square (always 9×9)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
size(S) :- S = 9.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  2. cells of the padded square
% ------------------------------------------------------------
cell(R,C) :- size(S), R = 0..S-1, C = 0..S-1.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  3. predicates for non‑zero rows and columns
% ------------------------------------------------------------
row_nonzero(R) :- input(R,_,V), V != 0.
col_nonzero(C) :- input(_,C,V), V != 0.

maxRowNZ(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max { R : row_nonzero(R) }.
maxColNZ(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : col_nonzero(C) }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  4. offsets (X and Y appear positively via their heads)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
offsetX(X) :- maxColNZ(Cmax), X = Cmax + 1.
offsetY(Y) :- maxRowNZ(Rmax), Y = S - Rmax - 1.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  5. copy positions – four copies per non‑zero input cell
% ------------------------------------------------------------
orig(R,C,V)   :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, cell(R,C).

right(R,C,V) :-
    input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
    offsetX(X),
    R = R0,
    C = C0 + X,
    C <= S-1,
    cell(R,C).

down(R,C,V) :-
    input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
    offsetY(Y),
    R = R0 + Y,
    C = C0,
    R <= S-1,
    cell(R,C).

swap(R,C,V) :-
    input(R0,C0,V), V != 0,
    offsetX(X),
    offsetY(Y),
    R = R0 + X,
    C = C0 + Y,
    R <= S-1,
    C <= S-1,
    cell(R,C).

% any source cell (non‑zero colour) is one of the four copies
source(R,C,V) :- orig(R,C,V).
source(R,C,V) :- right(R,C,V).
source(R,C,V) :- down(R,C,V).
source(R,C,V) :- swap(R,C,V).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  6. exactly one colour per cell (including colour 0)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,V) : colour(V) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  7. background colour 0 for cells that never receive a non‑zero source
% ------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not has_source(R,C).

has_source(R,C) :- source(R,C,V), V != 0.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  8. a cell may not be both 0 and non‑0
% ------------------------------------------------------------
:- output(R,C,0), output(R,C,V), V != 0.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  9. grounding (helps the solver)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
:- cell(R,C).
```
